lethal: When a creature is reduced to 0 @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP} or below due to lethal drain, it is instead reduced to half their maximum @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP} below zero @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP}. If this is not possible, their @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP} reduces to the lowest value it can go to. This counts as a @Wiki[Category:Killing effects]{killing effect}.
mana: Mana drain is similar to @Identifier[core:damage]{damage}, but it reduces @Wiki[Core:MP]{MP} instead of @Wiki[Core:HP]{HP}.
memory:
Memory drain is identical to @Identifier[drain:mana]{mana drain}, but it is not blocked by @Identifier[property:silver]{silver}. If you are @Identifier[status:down]{dropped} with Memory Drain then you forget the hour before you went @Identifier[status:down]{down}. If you are @Identifier[status:dead]{killed} by memory drain, you automatically forget all the events of the day prior to your @Identifier[status:dead]{death}. You pull from the @Identifier[keyword:death-bag]{death bag} as though you always have one additional black stone. Pulling one black stone will give you a @Identifier[keyword:death-bag]{death scar} and two will make you @Identifier[keyword:death-bag]{PD}. You always gain a permanent insanity from memory drain deaths determined by the @Identifier[core:game-master]{GM}.

Memory drain is considered a @Wiki[Category:Mental effects]{mental effect}, but is only partially stopped by @Wiki[Category:Protection keywords]{protections} such as @Identifier[ability:resist-mental-effects]{Resist Mental Effects}. They @Identifier[keyword:deboosted]{deboost} memory drain instead of causing it to fully @Identifier[core:fizzles]{fizzle}.
morganti: Morganti drain cannot be @Identifier[core:healing]{healed}. This includes @Identifier[status:dead]{death} and @Identifier[status:unconscious]{unconsciousness} imposed by it. If a creature is @Identifier[status:dead]{killed} with morganti damage then they cannot be @Wiki[Category:Revival effects]{revived}.
wither: If a creature takes Wither, then they age a number of years equal to the amount of Wither that they took. This aging is relative to a 100 year lifespan, regardless of how long the creature's lifespan would naturally be.
